markers.source_table: Suo et al. 2022; Miah et al. 2021
CL definition for GMP; Alsinet et al. 2022

other_findings.finding: Myeloid progenitors detected in prenatal thymus, spleen, skin, and kidney -- indicating system-wide haematopoiesis beyond conventional primary haematopoietic organs
Fetal liver produces the first GMPs and blood monocytes at 4-5 PCW
other_findings.source_table: Miah et al. 2021; Hoeffel and Ginhoux 2015

other_findings.finding: Myeloid progenitors from YSMP colonise the liver, contributing to fetal haematopoiesis until mid-pregnancy

evidence_quotes.quote: we detected B cell progenitors in almost all prenatal organs, megakaryocyte/erythroid progenitors in developing spleen and skin, and myeloid progenitors in the thymus, spleen, skin, and kidney
evidence_quotes.context: System-wide haematopoiesis in prenatal peripheral organs

evidence_quotes.quote: These HSCs rapidly enter the circulation and seed the fetal liver (FL), which produces the first population of granulocyte-monocyte progenitors (GMPs) and blood monocytes at 4-5 PCW
evidence_quotes.context: Origin of GMPs in fetal liver
